$comment: Documented-exception ledger for the parity closed loop. SOURCE OF TRUTH = the shots + specs generated from the design; any visual divergence is a BUG to fix by default. This holds ONLY narrow, documented exceptions, each governed by a doc. `exceptions` (FE-side): the FE intentionally does NOT match the mock — match by screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.kind == '*' OR entry.kind == finding kind); each needs a `source` citing a doc/owner ruling. `coverageExempt` (kit-side): kit nodes intentionally exempt from data-mx-node coverage. Keep MINIMAL — when in doubt, fix the FE to match the design.

exceptions.reason: The kit models each screen as its own MxScaffold (dashboard/screen); the FE factors the scaffold into the shared, persistent AppShell, so it cannot carry a per-screen ValueKey without rebuilding the whole shell on every tab switch. Chrome it provides (appbar/notifications/quick-review) IS keyed there when the Today tab is active.

The kit puts an app-bar overflow menu (import/export/settings) on the library screen. The FE consolidates those actions into the shared language Drawer (opened from the avatar), so there is no duplicate overflow control; adding one would create two paths to the same actions.

The kit shows an inline expanding search dock in the app bar. The FE routes the search button to a dedicated full-screen /search experience (richer filtering than an inline dock), so there is no in-place search-dock node.

The kit's empty library offers a secondary 'Add words' CTA. The FE data model requires a deck before any word can be added, so the empty state offers only 'Create deck' (empty-deck); an 'Add words' action is impossible with zero decks.

The kit deletes a single card via a confirm dialog launched from the card row. The FE manages cards (edit, hide, delete) inside the flashcard editor, not via a deck-detail row dialog, so there is no per-row delete-cancel/delete-ok here. (Deck deletion — deck-delete-* — IS keyed.)

The kit exposes a 'reset progress' confirm on deck-detail. v1 SRS scheduling is engine-driven (8-box Leitner) with no manual per-card/deck progress reset, so reset-cancel/reset-ok have no FE counterpart.

The kit plays card audio inline from the deck-detail row. The FE plays/generates audio inside the flashcard editor (audioSpeak), keeping the row a compact navigation target, so there is no row-level play-audio control.
The kit offers an in-screen search dock on deck-detail. The FE routes search to the dedicated global /search screen rather than a per-deck inline dock.
The kit's move flow has a separate 'apply' button. The FE move dialog applies immediately when a destination (root or a candidate deck) is tapped, so there is no distinct move-apply step.

The kit shows current/longest streak on the statistics screen. The FE surfaces streak (current + longest) on the dashboard Today tab where it drives engagement; the statistics screen focuses on library/SRS/accuracy/activity metrics and does not duplicate the streak counters.

The kit puts a user-profile section in settings. MemoX is a local-first app with no user account/identity; the only account surface is the optional Google Drive sync tile, so there is no profile node in settings.

The kit offers a single inline SRS-notification switch. The FE configures study reminders on a dedicated /reminder screen (enable + time + weekdays), which is richer than a single switch, so settings has a reminder navigation row instead of an inline toggle.

Stages 2-5 of NewLearn (and DueReview) reuse the shared W5 game widgets (recall/typing/guess/matching). The in-card controls reveal/check/hint/options/due-next/due-relearn are rendered by those widgets and carry their own game-* node identity, so the study session does not re-key them under study-session/*.
Control provided by the shared W5 typing game widget (game-typing/*), reused by the study session.
Control provided by the shared W5 game widgets, reused by the study session.
The multiple-choice options are provided by the shared W5 guess game widget (game-mc/*), reused by the study session.
DueReview grading (due-next / due-relearn) is provided by the shared recall game widget driving the SRS grade, not by study-session-owned buttons.
The kit has a dedicated resume-session prompt (resume-back/resume-retry). v1 has no mid-session resume prompt; an interrupted session simply re-enters fresh, so there is no resume state.

The kit splits load vs save failures into distinct recovery screens (save-error-back/retry). v1 surfaces any session load/persist failure as a single message state, so there are no separate save-error affordances.

The kit lets you edit a card inline while browsing (edit / edit-cancel / edit-save). Review is a strictly read-only browse that never mutates cards or the schedule (D-007); edits go through the flashcard editor screen, so there is no inline edit dialog.
The kit plays card audio from the review browser. Audio playback/generation lives in the flashcard editor; the review browse is read-only term+meaning, so there is no audio control here.
The kit offers a per-card options menu in review. The read-only browse has no per-card actions, so there is no options control.

The kit has an in-review text-size control. Font scale is a global personalization setting (applied live app-wide from the /theme screen), not a per-browse control, so review has no local text-size affordance.
The kit offers a playback-speed control. v1 auto-play advances on a fixed interval with no speed adjustment, so there is no speed control.
The kit offers a player options menu. v1 auto-play is a minimal term+meaning playback with no per-session options, so there is no options control.
Font scale is a global personalization setting (applied live app-wide from the /theme screen), not a per-player control.

The result is a finished-state of the study-session screen, not a separate route; it reuses the study-session MxScaffold and app bar (keyed study-session/appbar), so there is no separate study-result app bar.
The kit shows daily-goal progress on the session result. Goal status is surfaced on the dashboard Today tab; the result reports words + accuracy only.
The kit offers a 'review wrong answers' action on the result. v1 re-queues missed cards within the session itself (D-016) and keeps no post-session wrong list, so there is no review-wrong action.
The kit offers a 'finish later' action. v1 finishes the session in place (continue or to library); there is no defer-session action.
The kit splits SRS finalize failures into later/retry recovery. v1 persists SRS grades automatically as the session runs, so the result has no finalize-later/finalize-retry recovery UI.
The kit matching game has an explicit next control. The FE matching game auto-progresses — a correct pair disappears and the round advances on its own (D-015) — so there is no manual next button.
The kit offers a per-game options menu. The FE matching game is a minimal pair-the-tiles board with no in-game options control.
The kit guess game has a next control. The FE guess game auto-advances on answer (a tap grades and moves on, D-015), so there is no manual next button.
The kit plays prompt audio in the guess game. Audio lives in the flashcard editor; the game prompt is a minimal term display with no audio control.
The kit lets you edit the card from the guess game. Card editing is done in the flashcard editor, not mid-game, so there is no in-game edit control.
The kit recall game has a next control. The FE recall game auto-advances after self-grading (forgot/remembered grade and move on, D-015), so there is no manual next button.
The kit offers a per-game options menu. The FE recall game is a minimal reveal-and-grade card with no in-game options control.
Audio playback lives in the flashcard editor; the recall card is a minimal term/meaning reveal with no audio control.
Card editing is done in the flashcard editor, not mid-game, so there is no in-game edit control.
The kit fill-in game has a next control. The FE typing game auto-advances after a correct check or accept/retry (D-015), so there is no manual next button.
The kit offers a per-game options menu. The FE typing game is a minimal type-the-term card with hint/check/retry/accept controls and no options menu.

The kit has a discrete 'to preview' step. The FE import is a single page: once a source is picked/pasted, the column mapping and a live preview render inline, so there is no separate to-preview navigation step.

The kit splits export into generate then a separate save action. The FE writes the file directly when do-export runs (platform file-save / web download via FileSaveService), so there is no separate save button.
The kit offers a share action for the exported file. v1 export writes the file to disk (or downloads on web) with no OS share-sheet integration, so there is no share control.
